{'proved': True, 'checks': [{'name': 'main_uniqueness_theorem', 'passed': True, 'backend': 'kdrag', 'proof_type': 'certificate', 'details': 'kd.prove succeeded and returned a Proof object: |= ForAll([a11,
        a12,
        a13,
        a21,
        a22,
        a23,
        a31,
        a32,
        a33,
        x1,
        x2,
        x3],
       Implies(And(a11 > 0,
                   a22 > 0,
                   a33 > 0,
                   a12 < 0,
                   a13 < 0,
                   a21 < 0,
                   a23 < 0,
                   a31 < 0,
                   a32 < 0,
                   a11 + a12 + a13 > 0,
                   a21 + a22 + a23 > 0,
                   a31 + a32 + a33 > 0,
                   a11*x1 + a12*x2 + a13*x3 == 0,
                   a21*x1 + a22*x2 + a23*x3 == 0,
                   a31*x1 + a32*x2 + a33*x3 == 0),
               And(x1 == 0, x2 == 0, x3 == 0)))'}, {'name': 'numerical_sanity_example', 'passed': True, 'backend': 'numerical', 'proof_type': 'numerical', 'details': 'Concrete example matrix has row sums 1.0, 2.0, 3.0 and determinant 46.0; nonzero determinant confirms only the trivial solution.'}]}

{'proved': True, 'checks': [{'name': 'irreducible_for_all_n', 'passed': True, 'backend': 'kdrag', 'proof_type': 'certificate', 'details': 'kd.prove produced proof: |= ForAll([n, d],
